mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already low, the discount-rate channel dominates and equity valuations expand faster than earnings revisions deteriorate, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with FEDFUNDS declining on a 3-month basis, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above the prior 3-month trend forces real-rate expectations higher and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day forward returns more often than not, or if the sign of the effect flips when CPIAUCSL is above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS signals weakening household income growth and weaker future demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not produce negative 60-day returns in a majority of cases, or if UNRATE increases without PAYEMS slowing still predict positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in INDPRO indicates improving production and inventory restocking, supporting earnings expectations and lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month INDPRO changes do not align with higher average 20-day forward SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS grows faster than its recent trend, firms are realizing stronger nominal demand and revenue momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth exceeds its 12-month moving average by 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the thresholded growth signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns on average, or if its effect disappears after controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation trends.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in HOUST and RSAFS indicates both housing activity and consumer spending are strengthening, which should lift cyclicals and the broad index over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with both HOUST and RSAFS above their 6-month moving averages do not outperform baseline 60-day returns, or if either series alone explains the effect fully.

mechanism_narrative: In a soft-landing regime, a low and stable UNRATE supports risk appetite because labor incomes remain intact while inflation pressure eases, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE stays below 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is falling
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-unemployment and disinflation regime does not yield higher 5-day forward returns than the unconditional average, or if higher UNRATE offsets the effect.

mechanism_narrative: After FEDFUNDS remains above the estimated neutral range, equity prices should fall as discount rates stay restrictive and future growth gets re-rated lower, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if high-policy-rate episodes do not show below-baseline 60-day returns, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is stable and FEDFUNDS remains elevated.
